briefs.title: Chief Election Officer Recommended for 2026 Election
briefs.summary: Dennis Back is recommended to be the Chief Election Officer for the 2026 election. The Chief Election Officer is in charge of running the election.

briefs.title: North Road BIA in Burnaby Approved to Continue
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby has approved the renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA). This means the BIA will continue to operate and collect levies (fees) from businesses within its boundaries to fund local improvements and promotions.
briefs.action_required: No action is required unless you opposed the BIA renewal.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Professional Services, Hotel & Accommodation

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority: Approve Annual Shareholder Matters
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority needs approval for its yearly shareholder tasks. This is a standard part of keeping the organization running smoothly.
briefs.action_required: Review and approve the annual shareholder matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Authority Financial Statements and Budget Released
briefs.summary: The Burnaby Housing Authority (BHA) has released its financial statements for 2025 and its operating budget for 2026. This outlines how the BHA managed money last year and plans to spend it in the coming year.

briefs.title: E-Bike and Electric Kick Scooter Programs: Bylaw Changes Proposed
briefs.summary: Proposed changes to bylaws may affect e-bike share and electric kick scooter programs. The changes aim to update rules for these programs.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed bylaw changes to see if they impact your business.

briefs.title: Burnaby Housing Target Progress Report Released
briefs.summary: Burnaby has released a report on its progress towards meeting provincial housing targets. The report is for informational purposes and shows how the city is working to increase housing supply.

briefs.title: Building Benchmark BC Year 7 Participation Authorization
briefs.summary: Building Benchmark BC helps building owners measure and track their energy and water use. By participating, owners can identify ways to save money and reduce their environmental impact. This notice is to inform owners that they can seek authorization to participate in Year 7 of the program.
briefs.action_required: Seek authorization to participate in Building Benchmark BC Year 7.

briefs.title: FortisBC Gas Line Right-of-Way Approved for Pandora and Gaglardi
briefs.summary: FortisBC has been granted a Statutory Right-of-Way (legal permission to use land) to build and maintain gas lines at 7950 Pandora Street and 1200 Gaglardi Way. This ensures FortisBC can provide gas services to these locations.

briefs.title: Modular Housing Four-Plex Contract Awarded
briefs.summary: A contract has been awarded for a modular four-plex housing unit. This means the city is moving forward with a project to build this type of housing.

briefs.title: Culvert Replacement Contract Awarded
briefs.summary: A contract has been awarded for the Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement. This means the project will move forward with the selected contractor.

briefs.title: Central Valley Greenway: Contract Awarded for Culvert Replacement, Paving
briefs.summary: A contract has been awarded for culvert replacement and paving work on the Central Valley Greenway. This means construction work will be underway.

briefs.title: Cellular Voice and Data Services: Contract Change
briefs.summary: There is a contract change for cellular voice and data services. This affects the terms of your business's mobile phone plan.
briefs.action_required: Review the new contract terms for your cellular voice and data services.

briefs.title: HVAC System Maintenance Contract Change Requires Approval
briefs.summary: A change to your Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning (HVAC) system maintenance contract needs your approval. This ensures your business's heating and cooling systems are properly maintained.
briefs.action_required: Review and approve the HVAC system maintenance contract change.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Grocery & Food Retail, Healthcare, Spa & Wellness, Professional Services

briefs.title: Cisco WebEx Contact Center License Subscription Renewal
briefs.summary: Approval is needed to renew the Cisco WebEx Contact Center licenses. This ensures continued access to the WebEx Contact Center platform.
briefs.action_required: Review and approve the contract renewal for Cisco WebEx Contact Center licenses.

briefs.title: Fire Protection System Contract Renewal: Ensure Continued Service
briefs.summary: Your business needs working fire protection systems. This is a reminder to renew your service contract for these systems. Make sure your fire protection systems and equipment continue to be serviced without a break.
briefs.action_required: Review your fire protection system service contract and renew it before it expires.
briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant, Grocery & Food Retail, Healthcare, Spa & Wellness, Liquor, Cannabis, Construction & Development, Professional Services, Finance & Insurance, Technology, Natural Resources, Transportation & Logistics, Agriculture, Labour & Employment

briefs.title: City to Review Financial Practices with External Expert
briefs.summary: The city will hire an outside expert to review its financial practices. This is to make sure the city is handling money properly. The goal is to improve how the city manages its finances.

briefs.title: Beresford Street Rezoning Application in Burnaby
briefs.summary: A rezoning application for 5035 Beresford Street will be forwarded to the City of Burnaby for First and Second Reading. The application is for a 20-storey apartment building.
briefs.action_required: Monitor City of Burnaby updates on the rezoning application.

briefs.title: Developer Chooses to Forfeit Security Instead of Building Amenity Pier
briefs.summary: A developer has chosen to give up a security deposit instead of building an amenity pier. This means the pier will not be built. The money will likely go to the local government.

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Rezoning 5058 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby is considering a change to the zoning (rules about what kind of buildings can be built) at 5058 Imperial Street. The city council has read the rezoning bylaw for the first and second time.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Rezoning 6800 Lougheed Hwy
briefs.summary: Burnaby is reviewing a rezoning bylaw for 6800 Lougheed Hwy. The city council has held the first and second readings of the bylaw. Rezoning changes the rules about what kind of buildings and businesses can be in that area.
briefs.action_required: Monitor Burnaby's website for updates and public hearing dates.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Burnaby Considers Rezoning Springer Avenue Properties
briefs.summary: The City of Burnaby is considering a change to the zoning (rules about what kind of buildings can be built) for properties at 2209 and 2225 Springer Avenue. The city council has read the rezoning bylaw for the first and second time.
briefs.action_required: Check the city's website for updates and public hearing dates.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Parking Meter & EV Charger Bylaw Changes Approved
briefs.summary: A bylaw about parking meters and electric vehicle (EV) charging has been updated. The bylaw now includes the final changes that were approved.
briefs.action_required: Review parking meter and EV charging rules to ensure you are in compliance.

briefs.title: Fees and Charges Bylaw Amended
briefs.summary: The city has officially approved changes to its fees and charges bylaw. This bylaw sets out the costs for various municipal services and permits.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand any changes to fees relevant to your business.

briefs.title: Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw Amended
briefs.summary: The city has updated its bylaw (a local law) about how it enforces its bylaws. This change affects how the city handles violations and penalties for breaking local rules.
briefs.action_required: Review the updated bylaw to understand current enforcement procedures.
